label: LP-700-HID
info:
Prereq

sudo apt-get install libudev-dev librtlsdr-dev libusb-1.0-0-dev libpthread-stubs0-dev

npm install robertsLando/node-red-contrib-usbhid

sudo nano /etc/udev/rules.d/10-telepost.rules

# this works, but is very generic
KERNEL=="hidraw*",SUBSYSTEM=="hidraw", MODE:="777", GROUP="telepost"

sudo udevadm control --reload-rules
sudo groupadd -f telepost
sudo gpasswd -a $USER telepost

unplug/ replug

name: red trigger
info:
Build a REDTRG msg for tuya_bridge device.

REDTRG values are in range 2000-3000 (convention)

Note: it uses dp 102 (for  [Switch MS-104]). 
Change the code if your tuya-bridge device uses a different dp.

Binary to String

name: ReadMe (Logging to a CSV file)
info:
Logging Information

Alarms and Warnings are ALWAYS logged regardless
of the state of the logging "button"

Logging is enabled/disabled via the "button". 

Logging File System Location:
The file system path for the logging file, if enabled via the logging button on the UI is a Unix RPi path (/home/pi/spe_data_log.csv). If you are running Node Red server on any other platform and plan to use the logging capability, you will have to update the path in the "Append to Log" node.

Logging Interval (initialized in the "Initialize" function node:
Logging interval is determined by 2 parameters, the polling interval and the polling counter. 
- Polling interval: Located in node "Status Request" and currently 250ms
- logging_rate: Located in node "Initialize Polling" and set to 20
This results in a log every 20 polls or 5 seconds

Logging CSV Header:
Since logging appends to any previous log, including the header in the log is problematic. The following line of text can be imported into Excel as the header row and formated as desired:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------
Date,Time,Model,Operate/Standby,RX/TX,Bank,Input,Band,TX Ant,RX Ant,PWR LVL,PWR Out,ATU SWR,Ant SWR,PA V,PA I,Upper Temp,Lower Temp,Comb Temp,Warnings,Alarms
--------------------------------------------------------------------------

name: ReadMe (Power On Python Script Required)
info:
A python script is required for the Power On button. 

Prerequisites:
--------------------------------
 * Python (https://www.python.org/)
 * Python library modules
 *     serial 
 *     time
 * power_spe_on.py python script (provided below)

Background:
--------------------------------
The SPE has a documented method in the API to turn the SPE off with no documented method to turn it back
on. Research and assists from other hams have determined the following USB sequence can be used to turn
the SPE back on:
 * Set DTR Low
 * Set RTS High
 * Pause 1 second
 * Set DTR High
 * Set RTS Low
To date, I have not found a way to do this internal to Node Red. I have discovered this can be done with 
a Python script which is triggered via a Node Red "exec" command.

Python is available on both Unix (RPi) and Windows systems and the script appears to work on both operating
systems. In my case, I run Node Red on an RPi 4B installed per the Wiki located here:
    https://groups.io/g/nodered-hamradio/wiki
The package included Python and



Testing Python Installation and the power on script:
----------------------------------------------------

Verify Python is installed from a the Node Red server's command window as follows:
    Unix:
    ======================================
    pi@node-red:~ $ python
    Python 2.7.16 (default, Oct 10 2019, 22:02:15)
    [GCC 8.3.0] on linux2
    Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
    >>> exit()
    pi@node-red:~ $
    ======================================

    Windows:
    ======================================
    ======================================
    
If Python is not installed on your Node Red server, 
    Unix:
    ======================================
    - Update Pi: "sudo apt update"
    - Install Python: "sudo apt install python3 idle3"
    - Test Python installation per the above test procedure

    - Install PySerial: "sudo apt install python3-pip"
    - Teest PySerial: "python3 -m pip show pyserial"
    ======================================
    
    Windows
    ======================================
    - Download the Windows installer from https://www.python.org/downloads/
    - Run the installation script
    - Test Python installation per the above test procedure
    ======================================

Build the "power_spe_on.py" Script
    Unix:
    ======================================
    - Open the nano text editor, Nano will open on your /home/<username> directory:  "sudo nano"
    - Copy the power_spe_on.py script text below   
    - Paste the script into the nano editor, use the "edit" menu on the editor, "paste"
    - Change the "ser=....." line in the script, below, to match the id of your USB cable.
    - Write the file on the Nano editor:  Control "o"
    - Nano will ask for a file name, using the "edit" menu in Nano, Paste "power_spe_on.py" into Nano.
    - Then hit the "Return" key.
    - Confirm the file has been written
    - Exit the Nano Editor; Control x    
    ======================================

    Windows:
    ======================================
    - Navigate to ???? via File Explorer
    - Create a new text document in that directory: Right click -> New -> Text Document
    - Rename the text file to power_spe_on.py
    - Use Notepad to edit the text document: Right click -> Open with -> Notepad
    - Copy the power_spe_on.py script text below   
    - Paste the script into the nano editor, use the "Edit" menu on the editor, "Paste"
    - Change the "ser=....." line in the script, below, to match the id of your USB cable.
    - Write the file: use the "File" menu -> "Save as"
    - Exit the Notepad Editor: use the "File" menu -> "Exit"
    ======================================


#========power_spe_on.py Script Text=======================================================================
#  Note "#" characters in Python are comments and they can be included in the final script
#
#For Unix    systems, update the arguement in the "ser = ...." to reflect the Unix device path for the SPE
#For Windows systems, update the arguement in the "ser = ...." to reflect the COM port (e.g., COM14)
#WITHOUT any colon after the COM port name
#
#
import serial
import time
ser = serial.Serial("/dev/serial/by-id/usb-FTDI_FT232R_USB_UART_AI040UW5-if00-port0")
ser.dtr = 1
ser.dtr = 0
ser.rts = 1
time.sleep(1)
ser.dtr = 1
ser.rts = 0
ser.close()
exit()
#==========================================================================================================


Test the Script (Unix and Windows)
    - If not already connected, connect a USB cable between the Node Red server and the SPE
    - Open a command window and change the directory to the location of the Python power_spe_on.py script
    - Issue the following command:
        python power_spe_on.py
    - The SPE should power on
    
Troubleshooting:
    - A USB to DB-9 RS-232 cable (FTDI chipset) and an RS-232 break out box with LEDs on the control
      signals can be used to test the flow
    - Connect the USB to DB-9 RS-232 cable to the Node Red server
    - Connect the break out box to the end of the cable
    - Note the DTR and RTS LEDs
    - Run the script
    - The LEDs should follow the logic in the script

name: Initialization
Checksum
Wraparound
ReadMe (User Customization)
info:
User Customization:
--------------------------------
1) ***USB Port (**Required**)**
   Update the USB Request (labelled "SPE USB") node to reflect the correct USB path for your system
   tHE /dev/serial/by-id/....... is the preferred path rather than /dev/ttyUSBx as the former is plug in 
   order independent. This must also be updated in the python script below that is used to power the SPE
   on via the flow's power on button.
   
   **IMPORTANT: DO NOT CREATE A NEW SERIAL PORT AS THAT WILL RESULT IN FLOW INCOMPATIBLE SETTINGS.**
   
   ** Correct USB Serial Port Configuration:**
   a) Double click on the "SPE USB" serial request node
   b) Click on the small pencil icon
   c) Edit the "Serial Port" text box changing "/dev/serial/by-id/usb-......" to your serial port name
   
   If you accidentally update the settings, use the guide below to correct the important ones:
   - Settings: Baud Rate: 57600
   - Input, optionally wait for a start character of "0xAA" (without the quotation marks)
   - Input: Split input: "into fixed lengths of" "76" characters
   - Input: and deliver "binary buffers"
   - Request: Default response timeout "200" ms
   
2) **2K-FA port speed (Required)**
   Amplifier specific suggestions:
   - 2K-FA: set the baud rate to no faster than 38400

3) **Temperature Units (Required):**
   If SPE is configured for degrees Fahrenheit, edit the "Initialization" function node to select
   degrees F.

4) **SPE Power On Python Script (Optional)**
   The SPE Power On function requires Python and a python script in the home directory called
   "power_spe_on.py". Refer to the "Warning (Python Script Required) node for details.

5) **SPE Dashboard Widgets (Optional):**
   Three dashboard widgets are included in the flow:
   - SPE (Main dashboard widget)
   - SPE Auxiliary (Widget with lesser used functions and can be disabled via the user
   - SPE Test (Diagnostic widget and disabled by default)
   To disable a widget, click on the small triagle icon immediately below the hamburger menu,
   Find the widget in question (e.g., "SPE Auxiliary"), hover over it, select Edit and click the 
   "Disable" button (displayed as "Enabled" if currently enabled) at the bottom of the screen.

6) **Power Gauge Color Transitions and Maximum Reading (Optional):**
   Adjust the power gauge color transitions and maximums to your liking. See instructions in the
   "PWR Meter Range" function node.
   Edit the "Initialization" function node to select the desired values

7) **Exciter Drive Level (Optional):**
   This flow can define an exciter drive level which can then be linked via a "link in" node 
   to a third party radio flow. The feature follows the FlexRadio model where one defines the exciter
   drive power for each band and power level (standby, low, middle, maximum). If you plan on using this feature
   you must:
   a) Configure the table in the function node "Define Exciter Drive Power"
   b) Link the corresponding "link out" to a "link in" node in your third party radio flow
   c) A debug node is available for testing.
   PLEASE CHECK USER CONFIGURED DRIVE LEVELS ARE PROPERLY BEING PASSED TO THE EXCITER AS YOU
   RISK BLOWING UP YOUR AMP THE EXCITER FLOW DOES NOT PROPERLY PICK UP THE DRIVE LEVEL FROM 
   THIS (SPE) FLOW!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

8) **Fan Settings (Optional):**
   The fan setting (quiet or normal) buttons work on both 1.5K (VK4TUX) and 2K (WB2WGH). The
   flow does seem to leave the front panel in different display states between the two. This could be a
   firmware issue or a model specific issue. I have added A display command with 5 second delay per 
   VK4TUX's suggestion but left those nodes disabled. If your SPE does not return to its original states
   try enabling the 6 (3 per fan mode) disabled nodes. Refer to notes in the "ReadMe (Band Switching 
   and Fan Mode)" comment node.

9) **Tuner Out Link (Optional):**
   A Tuner Out link is provided to key up an exciter for tune functions. This link is disabled
   by default.

10) **Overtemperature Action (Optional):**
   Configure the overtemperature action per the instructions provided in the 
   "ReadMe (Overtemperature Configuration)" comment node.

11) **Set Exciter Drive Level (optional):**
   For ALC rigs, an optional link out is provided along with a function node to devine
   per band, per SPE power level (min, mid, max) exciter drive level. Refer to the function
   node "Define Exciter Drive Power" in the flow to configure the exciter drive levels. 
   This is NOT a replacement for ALC which SPE strongly recommends. It's more of a safety 
   net. As a Flex user, I have no way to test this.

name: Change History
info:
Outstanding Bugs and/or Issues:
--------------------------------

Future Enhancements:
--------------------------------
- Validate and update temperarature meter thresholds
- Correct the white foreground lettering on all the status indicators (model, T/R status, band, RX Ant,
  warnings, alarms, USB status). The list includes all boxes with rounded corners. The forground white is
  a dimmer white than on the actual buttons (square corners).
- Baank switching: validate the sequences
  for the 1.3K, 1.5K and 1.5K Taurus

Change History
--------------------------------
- 09/18/21: V0b1, Initial Baseline
- 10/19/21: V0b2 
-           -Added Bank Change functionality
-           -Added Tune functionality
-           -Changed background color of display -           items to blue as a differentiator
-           with buttons. Operate, power level
-           and other select buttons still 
-           change color when not at "base"
-           values.
- ??/??/??: V0b3
-           - Changed color scheme of Power     
-             Level. Green when in standby. In 
-             operate, low is yellow, middle is
-             yellow/red and maximum is red.
-           - Changed scaling on V PA meter
-           - Rearranged layout from 4 columns
-             wide to 8 columns wide shrinking
-             the vertical height for a more
-             compact layout.
-           - 6M band value returned as a
-             numeric and not as a string as
-             is the case with the lower bands.
-             Not sure about 4M band. Corrected
-             code for 6M.
-           - Changed buttons from button nodes to UI Template
-             Nodes allowing dual function widgets (status and
-             control) eliminating the need for status widget and
-             control widget). This reduces the dashboard 
-             footprint.
-           - ===Interim release to Nick, W7NIK, 1/16/2022
-           - Fixed "Tune" flow as Nick discovered my incomplete
-             implementation.
-           - ===Interim release to Nick, W2NIK, 1/18/2022
-           - Started testing Filter/RBE to minimize browser impact in
-             preparation for increased polling rate. At 19,200 baud, 
-             polls complete in about 40ms with assuming no
-             inter-character delay and no Node-Red latency. Any 
-             significant increase in polling rate will likely require
-             an increase in SPE baud rate (max=115,200)
-           - Installed individual Filter/RBE nodes on all indicators
-           - SPE bank selection only functions while in standby. Addeed
-             switch to only permit bank changes while in standby.
-           - Filter/RBE nodes messed up status displays and/or 
-             color on Bank (col5) and Power Level (col10). Removed
-             those filter/RBE nodes from the flow.
-           - Filter/RBE nodes associated with status displays (Model,
-             Mode, TX/RX, Band and RX Antenna) do not display correctly
-             (no text and default green color) after a browser refresh.
-           - Changed baud rate from 19,200 to 57,600
-           - Changed poll rate from 1 second to 250ms
-           - Changed SWR and Power from gauge to horizontal level
-           - Autorange power out meter based on SPE
-             model and power level setting
-           - ===Interim release to Nick, W2NIK, 1/26/2022
-           - Adjusted yellow & red voltate and current meter thresholds
-           - Added file logging capability (/home/pi/spe_data_log.txt). 
-             Logging every 20 samples (5 seconds at 250ms per poll) and
-             includes date/time stamp and all data fields returned from
-             the SPE in CSV format
-           - ===Interim release to Nick, W2NIK, 1/31/2022
-           - All warnings and alarms are logged regardless of the
-             logging button state.
- 02/10/22: V1.0
-           - Updated documentation
-             - rbe (run by exception) replaced by "filter" node and may
-               already be installed in your palette
            V1.1 Changes:
-           - Changed the Antenna button from
-               "ANT nt" where "n" is the antenna number and "t" is the ATU state (a=enabled, b=disabled)
-             to
-               "ANT n\nATU=state" where "\n" is a new line and "state" = ATU=ON or ATU=OFF 
-           - Added test to see if the SPE is resonding to polls (new switch node). This eliminates 
-             Node Red errors when the SPE is powered off
-           - Changed low power color from yellow to blue for improved contrast. This affects both low
-             and medium (half blue half red) settings
-           - ===Interim release to Alan, WA9WUD, 4/8/2022
-           - Renamed debug nodes to reflect content
-           - Eliminated RBE on the input to the "Decode Antenna" function as it sometimes results in
-             blank (no text) buttons
-           - Changed color scheme of Power     
-             Level. Green when in standby. In 
-             operate, low is purple, middle is
-             purple/red and maximum is red.
-           - SWR gauges display 2 decimal digits yet the Pretty ATU/ANT SWR function node only passed
-             1 decimal digit. Fixed the function nodes to pass 2 decimal digits.
-           - ===Interim release to Adrian VK4TUX and Steve K3FZT 4/18/2022
-           - Remamed the SPE serial nodes to elimate the /dev/ttyUSBx in the node's name as the 
-             name is purely textual and the /dev/ttyUSBx might cause confusion
-           - Added SPE USB communications status display
-           - Power Off button implemented. 
-           - Power on button implemented.
-             Based on testing with a USB RS232 FTDI cable and an RS232 break out box
-                - DTR steadu state high
-                - DTR Low   <<<<<<<<< SPE Windows GUI "On" button pressed
-                - RTS High
-                - Pause 1 second
-                - DTR High
-                - RTS Low
-           - Added support for metering in degrees C or F. 
-           - Corrected Units for volt and amp meters. Default of "Units" was replaced with "Volts" and
-             "Amps" respectively.
-           - Changed "SPE Responding" detection from is not null to msg.status == "OK"
-           - Changed Serial USB timeout from 500ms to 200ms. The default SPE USB serial polling rate
-             for the flow is 250ms which was selected as a compromise for responsiveness and loading.
-             If the SPE is non-responsive (e.g., powered off), the earlier 500ms timeout with a 250ms
-             polling rate would cause significant delays in the SPE UI when SPE was powered back on
-             resulting in long delays on the order of seconds to minutes. I suspect Node Red is
-             queueing the polls and must flush the outstanding (one that did not time out) polls
-             before any new button pushes are acquired. The fix was to ensure the poll injection rate
-             (250ms) is longer then the USB serial request timeout (changed from 500ms to 200ms).
-             
-             Note: After initial testing at 150ms, I saw flickering on the "USB STATUS", warnings and
-             alarms dashboard icons. Bumping up to 200ms eliminated this. 
-           - ===Interim release (V1.1b1) to Nick W7NIK, John W9KXQ, Angelo, I2DJX, Adrian VK4TUX and 
-             Steve K3FZT 4/25/2022
-   04/30/22: V1.1 Released
-           - Corrected copy/paste typos for the 1.3K-FA power gauge maximum and color transitions.
-           - Added comments in the Read Me and "PWR Meter Range" with guidance on adjusting gauge
-             color transitions and maximums.
-           - Added debug node after buffer to string conversion
-           - Cleaned up and deleted historical commented out code from assorted function nodes
-           - Added capability to define exciter drive power level. User must customize the "Define
-             Exciter Drive Power" function node.
-           - Reworked case statement for band display. As mentioned above, the SPE returns band data
-             as string and numeric depending on band. Rather than mix string and numeric comparisons
-             in the case statement, using parseInt() which always yeilds a numeric, regardless of
-             input type, prior to the case, eliminates this issue.
-           - ===Interim release (V1.2b1) tofor "node-red-node-serialport" in the read me

-           - If operating mode (standby/operate) comes back with an unexpected character, force the 
-             optional "Define Exciter Drive Power" to report zero since we don't know what state the
-             SPE is and we don't want to blow it up.
-           - Last byte of status messages were getting cut off (CR returned and LF dropped) due to
-             USB request node requesting 75 bytes instead of 76. Also changed "6, 76, Other Byte
-             Buffer?" switch node to for 76 byte buffers.
-           - As a FYI, even though the "Buffer Size?" switch node checks for smaller packets, they
-             never make it out of the "USB Request" node as with no consistent delimiter, a 76 byte 
-             fixed length query is used and all smaller confirmations will result in a timeout.
-           - Consolidated logging flow variable initialization into the master initialization node
-           - Changed buffer length and checksum validation along with "SPE Responding" logic to
-             eliminate "USB Status" indicator momentary red blips when changing SPE settings. The short
-             packets resulting from a change (e.g., change power level) result in a valid but short
-             packet with timeouts as the 76 byte length is not achieved. Logic has to account for that
-             in both SPE powered on and off states. 
-           - Flow layout cleanup (no functional changes)
-           - Discovered packets sometimes wrap around due to inability to detect start and end of frames. 
-             Landed on 0xAA for start with 76 byte packets (original design). Tried silence after first 
-             byte and others, none worked well. The addition of checksum detection raised the issue as 
-             bad frames previously went unnoticed. 
-           - Added wrap around detection to see how many of the bad frames were the result of wrap arounds.
-             76 byte status packets should look like AA AA AA ....... CR LF. Sometimes a sync issue
-             would occur and they would wrap around as AA AA ....... CR LF AA. 
-           - Reduced V PA gauge minimum value from 35V to 30V to accommodate the 2K-FA
-           - ===Interim release (V1.2b2) to Nick Adrian VK4TUX

-           - Added checksum status indicator and shrunk USB Status indicator to make room
-           - Changed the checksum status indicator to a button to allow users to force a recovery from
-             buffer wrap around conditions. 
-           - Disable hot switching from standby to operate while in TX mode
-           - Disable hot switching power on to power off while in TX mode
-           - Disable hot switching of power level (low, middle, maximum) while in TX mode
-           - Added clarification on displaying C or F inside the Initialization node
-           - ===Interim release (V1.2b3) (WB2WGH internal release)

-           - Added the "SPE Auxiliary" dashboard widget with additional controls:
-              Backline Off/On
-              L-/+
-              C-/+
-              Set
-              Left/Right
-           - ===Interim release (V1.2b4) to Adrian (VK4TUX) and Andrea Fruch (IW3SSD)

-           - Added the "Display" button
-           - ===Interim release (V1.2b5) to Adrian (VK4TUX) and Andrea Fruch (IW3SSD)

-           - Deleted the legacy testing wraparound LED
-           - Split Read Me node into 3 nodes
-           - Added 76 Byte Buffer debug node
-           - Added instructions to install pyserial for the python script
-           - Added some Node Red groups for bank selection and SPE Auxiliary widget
-           - Added Fan mode (quiet and normal)
-           - ===Interim release (V1.2b6) to Nick (W7NIK)

-           - Renamed "CS STAT" button to "USB SYNC" button
-           - Added "BAND -" and "BAND +" to the SPE Aux widget
-           - ===Interim release (V1.2b7)

-           - Renamed node Comms Status to USB Status for consistency
-           - Added a link out for overtemperature to allow shutdown of the exciter per VK4TUX's
-             suggestion. Note 2 things:
-             - The state (operate or standby) of the SPE is not changed to avoid hot switching
-             - The nodes are are disabled and must be enabled if the user desires this feature
-           - Added "Tune" link out per VK4TUX's suggestion. The link node is disabled by default.
-           - ===Interim release (V1.2b8)

-           - Changed style of all display indicators (not buttons) to include 
-             "color: white; " so the text forground color now matches the button
-             forground color.
-           - Consolidated Dave's Head CSS and the SPE specific CSS into a SPE flow specific
-             CSS.
-           - Renamed the CSS styles to align to the best practices Wiki document authored by
-             Mick, W8BE. All style names are now of the form SPE__________
-           - ===Interim release (V1.2b9)
-   01/26/24: V1.2 Released

-           - Account for both 75 and 76 byte buffers. For some reason, USB Serial on some systems
-             pulls in 75 bytes.
-           - Tuck model returned in a flow context variable for access by the fan sequencing logic
-           - Totally reworked fan flow
-           - New palette requirement: node-red-contrib-loop

-           - ===Interim release (V1.3b1)

-           - For SPE Power On which requires the Python script, I disable the connection to the 
-             serial port before calling the Python script and re-enable it after return from the 
-             script. This resolves an issue reported by Vinod, where Node Red's CPU utilitiation 
-             increased after calling the script. This must be an issue with multiple processes 
-             accessing the same resource (serial port) concurrently. 
-           - Added polling lockout to prevent polling while menu operations (e.g., fan speed,
-             bank selection, etc.) are in process. This was done as a precaution given the use
-             of menu operations might interfere with standard polling and user initiated operations
-             (e.g, standby/operate, power level, etc)
-           - Rearranged polling nodes for aesthetics (no functional changes)
-           - Added "1.5K Taurus" as a recognized model and initially set gauges to mirror the 1.5K non-Taurus
-           - Cleanup up a number of the CSV decode function nodes to eliminate the use of the confusing variaable
-             "State"
-           - Disabled bank select on models without the bank select feature
-           - Added "Up" to "Left" button and "Down" to "Right" button (now "Left/Up" and "Right/Down")
-           - Removed selected RBE (filter) nodes (Temerature (upper, lower & 
-             combiner, Input) for flow reliability
-           - Added overtemperature features:
-                 - 4 Overtemperature modes:
-                     - 1) No action
-                     - 2) SPE return to standby from operate
-                     - 3) Exciter return to RX (via link out)
-                     - 4) Both 2 & 3 
-                 - Visual indicator (color and text) on Standby/Operate button for overtemperature state
-           - Changed names of "Warning" comment nodes to "ReadMe"
-           - Added diagnostic injects via an "SPE Test" dashboard widget that should be disabled for 
-             production use

-           - ===Interim release (V1.3b2)

-           - Added .SPEmedfont to the CSS as  16px;
-           - Changed Model to use SPEmedfont
-           - Split the Taurus model text into 2 lines
-           - Changed all SPE Auxiliary items to use SPEmedfont
-           - Split SPE Auxiliary buttons (left/up, right/down and fan) to 2 lines of text
-           - Cleaned up case statement layout in Fan Speed "Form Command Sequence" function node. Cosmetic
-             with no functional impact.
-           - Gray out fan buttons for models with unknown fan speed menu sequence
-           - Added text to fan buttons for models with unknown fan speed menu sequence
-           - In fan mode section, added some debug nodes and commented out model over rides in 3
-             function nodes for testing.
-           - 1.3b1 deployment had O/T mode set to spe_standby by mistake.
-           - Display Overtemperature Action state to the SPE Auxiliary menu
-           - Cleaned up some O/T test code left enabled by mistake
-           - Disable fan action for gray (N/A) buttons

-           - ===Interim release (V1.3b3)
-           - Added fan sequence for 1.5K Taurus based on input from Vinod, LB9KJ/VU3ESV
-           - Added comment to initialization function node regarding setting of the overtemperature
-             threshold other than the default of the gauge maximum. In the commented out
-             code to override (35 degrees) I indicated it serves 2 purposes, low temperature
-             testing and also to select a different value than the gauge maximum
-           - Corrected typo in "threshold" (was "threshhold") in comments and flow variables
-           - Added O/T threshold temperature to the O/T Action display in the SPE Auxiliary 
-             widget

-           - ===Interim release (V1.3b4)
-   11/23/25: V1.3 Released

name: ReadMe (Overview & Required Nodes)
info:
Ron Panetta, WB2WGH (ron@panman.us)

Overview:
--------------------------------
This flow provides command/control and monitoring of many of the SPE functions. Two different style icons
are used in the dashboard:
    - Rectangles with square corners: Buttons that can be used to change the state of the SPE. All 
      buttons have a hover feature to reinforce use as a button
    - Rectangles with rounded corners are status indicators and provide no control functionality.

Most status and buttons are intuitive as to function. A few require explaination:
    - BANK: Bank switching is only available on the 1.3K and 1.5K. The 2K does not have bank switching
      and as such, always reports "BANK X".
    - LOGGING ON/OFF: Reference the Read Me node regarding logging. This allows data provided by the SPE
      to be logged to a CSV file.
    - USB STAT: provides status of the USB polling of the SPE. If polling should fail (e.g., the SPE is off)
      this indicator will turn red, otherwise it will be green indicating successful polling of the SPE
    - USB SYNC: Checksum status provides an indication if status messages pass (green) or fail (red) checksum
      validation. Due to the SPE's API and Node Red limitations, it is possible for the SPE status messages
      to get into an wrap around situation. For additional insight see the read me notes below. Should this
      occur, the USB STAT button will turn red as is the case when the SPE is powered off. Pressing the 
      USB SYNC button will usually clear the issue.
      It should be noted that while the USB STAT button is red, the dashboard may not correctly present the
      state of the SPE. For example, if red, and one uses the front panel of the SPE to change a parameter
      (e.g., power level, operate/standby, etc.) the dashboard will not update to reflect those changes.
    
Usage:
--------------------------------
1) For Series II Expert Linear SPE 1.3K-FA, 1.5K-FA & 2K-FA (testing only perofrmed on the 1.5K and 2K units)
   Several 1.3K users are successfully using this flow.
2) Temperature meter thresholds are a "dart throw"

Required Palette Components
--------------------------------
1) node-red-contrib-ui-level (V0.1.46)
2) node-red-dashboard (V3.1.0)
3) node-red-node-serialport (V1.0.3)
4) node-red-contrib-loop (V1.0.1) (new requirement with SPE flow V1.3)
5) node-red-node-rbe (replaced by the "filter" node and may already be part of your palette)

Required Miscellaneous Components
--------------------------------
None at this time

Disable the following test/diagnostic groups:
    - Diagnostic Injects to Simulate Environment for Testing
    - Polling Lockout Diagnostic Trigger
    - Unsuccessful test of SPE Power On via Node Red

Reset the following flow variables in the "Initialization" function Node:
    - flow.set("overtemperature_action", "none"             )

Disable the following widgets in the Dashboard Layout menu:
    - SPE Test

Debug messages
    - Ensure debug output for the flow is clean
    - Disable any active debug nodes

SPE GitHub CSS
format:
<style>
:root{
  --gh-bg:#0d1117;--gh-card:#161b22;--gh-border:#30363d;
  --gh-text:#c9d1d9;--gh-muted:#8b949e;
  --gh-green:#3fb950;--gh-red:#f85149;--gh-amber:#e3b341;--gh-blue:#58a6ff;
  --gh-font:'SFMono-Regular','Consolas',monospace;
  --gh-sans:-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,'Segoe UI',sans-serif;
}
.gh-card{background:var(--gh-card);border:1px solid var(--gh-border);border-radius:6px;padding:10px 12px;font-family:var(--gh-sans);color:var(--gh-text);}
.gh-sec{font-size:10px;color:var(--gh-muted);letter-spacing:2px;text-transform:uppercase;padding:2px 0 5px;border-bottom:1px solid var(--gh-border);margin-bottom:8px;}
.gh-lbl{font-size:9px;color:var(--gh-muted);letter-spacing:1px;text-transform:uppercase;}
.gh-val{font-family:var(--gh-font);font-size:14px;font-weight:700;margin-top:2px;}
.gh-box{background:#0d1117;border:1px solid var(--gh-border);border-radius:4px;padding:5px 7px;}
.gh-track{background:#21262d;border-radius:2px;height:6px;width:100%;}
.gh-fill{height:6px;border-radius:2px;}
.gh-badge{border-radius:4px;padding:2px 8px;font-size:10px;font-family:var(--gh-font);display:inline-block;margin-top:2px;border:1px solid;}
.gh-ok{background:#0d2818;border-color:var(--gh-green);color:var(--gh-green);}
.gh-err{background:#2d0808;border-color:var(--gh-red);color:var(--gh-red);}
.gh-inf{background:#0d1a2a;border-color:var(--gh-blue);color:var(--gh-blue);}
.gh-wrn{background:#2d1800;border-color:var(--gh-amber);color:var(--gh-amber);}
.gh-muted-badge{background:#161b22;border-color:var(--gh-border);color:var(--gh-muted);}
.gh-btn{border-radius:4px;padding:6px 10px;font-size:11px;font-family:var(--gh-font);cursor:pointer;text-align:center;border:1px solid var(--gh-border);background:#0d1117;color:var(--gh-text);width:100%;transition:all 0.15s;}
.gh-btn:hover{border-color:var(--gh-blue);color:var(--gh-blue);}
.gh-btn-g{border-color:var(--gh-green);color:var(--gh-green);background:#0d2818;}
.gh-btn-r{border-color:var(--gh-red);color:var(--gh-red);background:#2d0808;}
.gh-btn-b{border-color:var(--gh-blue);color:var(--gh-blue);background:#0d1a2a;}
.gh-btn-a{border-color:var(--gh-amber);color:var(--gh-amber);background:#2d1800;}
.gh-exp-hdr{display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:space-between;cursor:pointer;padding:6px 0;border-top:1px solid var(--gh-border);margin-top:8px;user-select:none;}
.gh-exp-hdr:hover .gh-exp-lbl{color:var(--gh-blue);}
.gh-exp-lbl{font-size:10px;color:var(--gh-muted);letter-spacing:2px;text-transform:uppercase;}
.gh-exp-arr{font-size:10px;color:var(--gh-muted);transition:transform 0.2s;display:inline-block;}
.gh-exp-arr.open{transform:rotate(90deg);}
.gh-exp-body{overflow:hidden;max-height:0;transition:max-height 0.25s ease;}
.gh-exp-body.open{max-height:600px;padding-top:8px;}
</style>

name: Parse Current
Parse 24-Hour
Parse Predicted
func: const color=[] color[0]=env.get('COLOR0')||'#6EDC5A' color[1]=env.get('COLOR1')||'#F6EB15' color[2]=env.get('COLOR2')||'#FEC801' color[3]=env.get('COLOR3')||'#FD9600' color[4]=env.get('COLOR4')||'#FD2A00' color[5]=env.get('COLOR5')||'#C81F00' let rMinor=msg.payload[1].R.MinorProb!==null?Number(msg.payload[1].R.MinorProb):-1 let rMajor=msg.payload[1].R.MajorProb!==null?Number(msg.payload[1].R.MajorProb):-1 let sProb=msg.payload[1].S.Prob!==null?Number(msg.payload[1].S.Prob):-1 let gPred=msg.payload[1].G.Scale!==null?Number(msg.payload[1].G.Scale):-1 if((rMajor>=0&&rMajor<=100)||(rMinor>=0&&rMinor<=100)){if(rMajor>=50.0&&rMajor>rMinor+5){if(rMajor>=90.0){msg.payload="R⅗  "}else if(rMajor>=75.0){msg.payload="R⅗  "}else{msg.payload="R⅗  "}}else if(rMinor>=45.0){if(Math.abs(rMajor-rMinor)<=5){msg.payload="R⅔  "}else{msg.payload="R½  "}}else{msg.payload="R0  "}}else{msg.payload+='R?  '} if(sProb>=0&&sProb<=100){if(sProb>=90.0){msg.payload+="S⅕  "}else if(sProb>=80.0){msg.payload+="S⅕  "}else if(sProb>=70.0){msg.payload+="S⅕  "}else if(sProb>=60.0){msg.payload+="S⅕  "}else if(sProb>=50.0){msg.payload+="S⅕  "}else{msg.payload+="S0  "}}else{msg.payload+='S?  '} if(gPred>=0&&gPred<=5){msg.payload+='G'+gPred.toString()+''}else{msg.payload='G?'} return msg
